BombVault v4.1 adds a guided **disaster-recovery** flow — recovering onto a fresh or rebuilt server is now a walkthrough instead of a scavenger hunt across Settings, Containers and VMs.

## Added

- **Guided Recovery tab.** A dedicated **Recovery** tab that hand-holds the "server died, start over" case in one place:
  - **Connection check** — verifies BombVault can read your existing backups and, if the encryption key doesn't match, tells you exactly what to fix (set the original `APP_KEY` from your recovery kit in the container template). This is the one gotcha that trips up recovery, surfaced first.
  - **Attach your backups** — one consolidated panel to point at your existing repo, local or off-site (rest / S3 / B2 / sftp / rclone), with the credential and encryption fields right there (no hunting behind the Advanced toggle in a disaster).
  - **Discover** — one click rebuilds the list of containers and VMs stored in the backups, even with no live originals present; a real read failure now shows the actual error instead of "nothing found".
  - **Review & restore all** — restores every discovered container and VM, one at a time and **left stopped** so nothing races ahead of its dependencies, using the v4 live restore progress and cancel. Restore each one individually if you prefer.
  - **Recovery kit** — your standalone-restore safety net, one click away.
- **Fresh-install nudge** — a new or rebuilt install that has never backed up anything is offered a link straight to the Recovery tab from the Dashboard.

Recovery is pure orchestration over capabilities BombVault already had (definitions stored in the backup, attach-any-repo, restore-after-deletion for containers and VMs) — no change to the backup or restore engine. Fully translated across all 24 additional languages.

---
